name: Daejeon (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity and the optimistic future of the city
the rivers and sky of Daejeon and the city's spirit of science and progress
the warmth and energy of the citizens
the surrounding mountains and the harmony of nature and city

description: A white field charged with a stylised, multicolour emblem combining a wave-like form, the kanji-derived form of the character 大 (big) and an arc, with the bold blue wordmark 'Daejeon' beneath.
history: Daejeon, a Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) since 1989, adopted its current flag and city emblem in 1997. The four-colour mark combines stylised abstractions of the city's name (대전 / 大田, 'Great Field'), the surrounding mountains, the rivers that converge at Daejeon and a rising sun, expressing the city's identity as the heart of Korea's research and high-technology sector.

features.symbolism: abstract motif blending stylised forms of the character 大 ('great', the first kanji of 大田/Daejeon), the rivers that converge in the city, the surrounding mountains and a rising sun, expressing Daejeon's role as the centre of Korean science and technology
